headline: Suicide Rate Rises to 8.4 Per 100,000 With Youth Most Affected
context: Malaysia's suicide rate increased to 8.4 per 100,000 population in 2025 with the highest rates among 18-24 year olds.

cards.big: Suicide rate reached 8.4 per 100,000 in 2025 with 2,772 deaths recorded
cards.sub: Youth aged 18-24 account for 28% of all suicides the highest of any age group

cards.big: Malaysia decriminalized suicide attempts only in 2023 under Section 309 Penal Code amendment
cards.sub: Residual stigma from decades of criminalization still deters help-seeking behavior

cards.big: National crisis hotline Befrienders receives 45,000 calls annually but can answer only 60%
cards.sub: Volunteer-dependent model cannot scale to meet growing demand for crisis support

cards.big: Japan reduced suicide rate 38% through workplace mental health mandates and community gatekeepers
cards.sub: Multi-sector approach addressing root causes proved more effective than hotlines alone

cards.big: Every suicide represents a person who could not access help in time—not a personal failure
Should Malaysia establish a government-funded 24-hour national mental health crisis line
